issues.title: [coord] chore(memory): refresh project-memory to reflect post-PR#6 state
issues.body:
## Goal
Memory files pre-date PR #6. Main now has DB repos, auth, provider-connections, Sentry, and write endpoints. Agents picking tasks will act on stale facts unless memory matches `main` at `f46f599`.

## Acceptance criteria
- `03-current-state.md` reflects current main: Track A + Track B merged, DB-gated routes with in-memory fallback, Sentry wired.
- `05-api-map.md` lists all current endpoints: GETs + POSTs for projects/workspaces/chat-windows/messages, `/v1/auth/*`, `/v1/provider-connections`, `/v1/state`, `/v1/dev`.
- `07-backend-map.md` documents new dirs: `db/`, `providers/`, `lib/db.ts`, new controllers/services.
- `06-frontend-map.md` marks web as read-only + local-state (no write clients yet).
- `08-recent-changes.md` appends commits `dbd40ef`, `87beb1c`, `c8e3c26`, `f46f599`.
- `04-active-tasks.md` replaced with pointer to GitHub Project.

## Out of scope
- Writing code.
- Touching `docs/technical/`.

## Test plan
- `grep -r "read-only V1\|integration-homepage-projects\|19 tests" project-memory/` returns empty.
- `pnpm typecheck` unchanged.

issues.title: [coord] chore(env): commit .env.example for api and web
issues.body:
## Goal
No env template exists. Every downstream backend task (DB, auth, providers, Sentry) needs these var names documented before agents can run locally or wire CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/api/.env.example` includes every var read by `apps/api/src/config/env.ts` plus: `DATABASE_URL`, `PROVIDER_KEY_SECRET`, `SENTRY_DSN`, `SESSION_COOKIE_NAME`.
- `apps/web/.env.example` includes `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN`.
- Both files committed, no real secrets, placeholder values.
- `apps/api/README.md` and root `README.md` reference them.

## Out of scope
- Loading `.env` in code (already handled).
- Secret management tooling.

## Test plan
- Fresh clone, copy `.env.example`, `pnpm dev` boots api + web without crash in in-memory mode.

issues.title: [backend] chore(db): drizzle config + migration generation
issues.body:
## Goal
`apps/api/src/db/schema.ts` exists but no `drizzle.config.ts` and no generated SQL migrations are committed. Blocks every other DB-dependent task.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/api/drizzle.config.ts` pointed at `src/db/schema.ts`, output dir `apps/api/drizzle/`.
- `pnpm --filter @webapp/api db:generate` produces migration files, committed.
- `pnpm --filter @webapp/api db:migrate` applies cleanly on empty Postgres.
- All enums + tables from schema present after migrate (`users`, `sessions`, `projects`, `workspaces`, `chat_windows`, `messages`, `provider_connections`).

## Out of scope
- Docker-compose wiring (separate issue).
- Seed data.
- CI pg service.

## Test plan
- Empty local pg, `db:migrate`, `\dt` lists all 7 tables + enums.
- Re-run `db:migrate`, idempotent, no error.

issues.title: [backend] chore(infra): docker-compose postgres service + local bootstrap
issues.body:
## Goal
Without a reachable Postgres, DB-gated routes stay disabled and no DB work is testable end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria
- `docker-compose.yml` defines `postgres:16` service, port 5432, env matches `.env.example`.
- `DATABASE_URL=postgres://...@localhost:5432/webapp` boots api with DB mode active.
- README documents `docker compose up -d postgres && pnpm --filter @webapp/api db:migrate`.
- `/health` returns 200 with DB present.

## Out of scope
- Prod infra.
- Backups.

## Test plan
- `docker compose up -d postgres`, api boot with `DATABASE_URL`, `/v1/projects` hits DB route (verified via log).
- `unset DATABASE_URL`, api still boots, in-memory fallback serves.

issues.title: [coord] chore(types): publish write-path DTOs in @webapp/types
issues.body:
## Goal
Backend and frontend will grow write paths in parallel. Without shared DTOs they drift and we debug type mismatches instead of shipping.

## Acceptance criteria
- `@webapp/types` exports: `CreateProjectInput`, `CreateWorkspaceInput`, `CreateChatWindowInput`, `CreateMessageInput`, `AuthRegisterInput`, `AuthLoginInput`, `ProviderConnectionInput`, `ProviderConnectionPublic`, `MeResponse`.
- Re-exported from `packages/types/src/index.ts`.
- No new runtime deps; types only.
- `pnpm typecheck` green across monorepo.

## Out of scope
- Zod schemas (separate issue).
- Backend controller refactor to use them (separate issue).

## Test plan
- `pnpm --filter @webapp/types build`.
- `pnpm typecheck` green.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): encrypt provider api keys at rest (AES-256-GCM)
issues.body:
## Goal
User API keys must never be stored plaintext. Current `provider_connections` schema must guarantee ciphertext-at-rest before any UI lets users submit keys.

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration: `provider_connections.api_key_ciphertext` column (text). Drop plaintext column if present.
- `apps/api/src/lib/crypto.ts` AES-256-GCM with IV per record, key from `PROVIDER_KEY_SECRET` env.
- `provider-key.service` encrypts on write, decrypts only inside a provider-call path.
- Boot fails in `NODE_ENV=production` if `PROVIDER_KEY_SECRET` missing.
- No endpoint ever returns plaintext (masked or full).
- Test asserts raw DB row contains ciphertext different from input.

## Out of scope
- Key rotation flow.
- KMS integration.
- Audit log.

## Test plan
- Vitest: round-trip encrypt/decrypt; tampered ciphertext throws.
- Integration: POST connection, SELECT raw row, not plaintext.
- Boot prod without secret, process exits with clear message.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): POST message â†’ OpenAI chat-completion loop
issues.body:
## Goal
`POST /v1/messages` currently persists only the user message. Closing the loop (call provider, persist assistant reply) is the minimum viable product.

## Acceptance criteria
- On `POST /v1/messages` with `role='user'`, controller: persists user msg, loads chat-window + provider connection (decrypt key), loads prior messages in order, calls `openaiProvider.chat({ model, messages })`, persists assistant msg, returns both.
- Missing provider connection for window's provider returns 412 `provider_not_configured`.
- Provider error: assistant msg NOT persisted, 502 `provider_error`.
- Timeout 30s.
- Non-`user` role returns 400.
- Vitest with stubbed provider covers happy path, missing conn, timeout, error.

## Out of scope
- Streaming (SSE).
- Anthropic/Perplexity providers.
- Token/cost accounting.
- Retries.

## Test plan
- Vitest: 4 branches above.
- Manual: real key, POST, assistant row appears in DB.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): POST message client + optimistic render
issues.body:
## Goal
`useChatSessions` keeps messages in local state only. Once backend loop lands, UI must POST and render the persisted assistant reply.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lib/api/messages.ts` adds `postMessage(windowId, { role:'user', content })`.
- Composer submit: optimistic user msg (temp id), POST, replace id + append assistant msg on success; mark failed with retry on error.
- In-flight spinner; `AbortController` cancel button.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` unset: local-echo mock preserved.

## Out of scope
- SSE streaming.
- Edit/delete/resend flow.
- File uploads.

## Test plan
- Playwright vs stub backend: send, assistant renders.
- Force network fail, error state + retry.
- Mock mode unaffected.

issues.title: [backend] feat(auth): session middleware + GET /v1/me
issues.body:
## Goal
Consolidate auth into a single middleware that attaches `userId` to `RequestContext`, and expose `/v1/me` so frontend can verify session.

## Acceptance criteria
- `middleware/auth.ts` reads session cookie, loads session+user, attaches `ctx.userId`.
- `GET /v1/me` returns `{ id, email, displayName }` or 401.
- Existing `*-db.controller.ts` read `ctx.userId` instead of re-fetching.
- Tests: no cookieâ†’401, validâ†’200, expiredâ†’401 + session deleted.

## Out of scope
- CSRF (separate).
- Rate limiting (separate).

issues.title: [backend] feat(auth): CSRF protection on mutating routes
issues.body:
## Goal
Cookie-based auth needs CSRF defense for POST/PUT/DELETE.

## Acceptance criteria
- SameSite=Lax cookie + Origin header check on mutating methods.
- Bad/missing origin returns 403 `csrf_rejected`.
- Documented in api README.

## Out of scope
- Double-submit token scheme.

issues.title: [backend] feat(api): body validation for all POST controllers
issues.body:
## Goal
Write endpoints can 500 on bad JSON or wrong field types. Need consistent schema validation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Shared `parseJsonBody<T>(ctx, schema)` helper in `lib/http.ts`.
- Every POST controller uses it.
- Bad body returns 400 `invalid_body` with field list.

## Out of scope
- Replacing the custom router with a framework.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): provider-connections CRUD + metadata validation
issues.body:
## Goal
`provider-connections` routes exist but need full CRUD + metadata validation (provider in union, label length, uniqueness per user).

## Acceptance criteria
- GET list returns `{ id, provider, label, createdAt, lastUsedAt }` â€” never the key.
- POST validates provider in `AIProvider`, label 1â€“64 chars.
- DELETE scoped to current user.
- PUT renames label.
- Tests cover ownership (user A cannot touch user B's row).

## Out of scope
- Key rotation.
- Org-shared connections.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): POST /v1/provider-connections/:id/test
issues.body:
## Goal
Let users verify a key works before relying on it in a chat window.

## Acceptance criteria
- Endpoint calls provider's cheapest ping (e.g. `/models`).
- Returns `{ ok: true }` or `{ ok: false, code, message }`.
- Rate-limit 1/min per connection.

## Out of scope
- Model auto-discovery.

issues.title: [backend] test(api): CI ephemeral Postgres for DB route tests
issues.body:
## Goal
`*-db.test.ts` only run with `DATABASE_URL`. CI must spin pg and execute them.

## Acceptance criteria
- CI job `api-db-tests` uses `services: postgres`.
- Runs `db:migrate` then `pnpm --filter @webapp/api test`.
- README documents local `DATABASE_URL` for full suite.

## Out of scope
- Web e2e pg usage.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): Anthropic adapter
issues.body:
## Goal
Second provider adapter mirroring OpenAI contract.

## Acceptance criteria
- `providers/anthropic.provider.ts` + test.
- Registered in provider factory keyed by `AIProvider`.
- `POST /v1/messages` works for windows with `provider='anthropic'`.

## Out of scope
- Streaming, tool use.

issues.title: [backend] feat(providers): Perplexity adapter
issues.body:
## Goal
Third provider adapter.

## Acceptance criteria
- `providers/perplexity.provider.ts` + test.
- Handles citations (store in message metadata or drop â€” document in ADR).
- Registered in factory.

## Out of scope
- Citation rendering UI.

issues.title: [backend] feat(api): SSE streaming endpoint for messages
issues.body:
## Goal
Progressive token rendering for long replies.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /v1/chat-windows/:id/messages/stream` emits `text/event-stream`.
- Persists assistant msg on done, emits `event: done` + id.
- Client disconnect aborts provider call.

## Out of scope
- WebSocket transport.
- Frontend wiring.

issues.title: [backend] feat(auth): rate limit /v1/auth/login and /register
issues.body:
## Goal
Brute-force defense on auth.

## Acceptance criteria
- In-memory token bucket (swap-in interface for Redis).
- 429 after N failures per IP.
- Vitest covers limit + reset.

## Out of scope
- Global rate limiter.

issues.title: [backend] refactor(api): collapse in-memory + db controller duplication
issues.body:
## Goal
`*.controller.ts` and `*-db.controller.ts` duplicate logic. Services should expose one interface with two impls.

## Acceptance criteria
- `services/*.service.ts` expose an interface; in-memory + DB are impls.
- One controller per resource.
- Tests pass in both modes.

## Out of scope
- Removing fallback.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): login + register pages consuming /v1/auth/*
issues.body:
## Goal
UI entry points for existing auth endpoints.

## Acceptance criteria
- `src/app/login/page.tsx`, `src/app/register/page.tsx`.
- `lib/api/auth.ts`: `login`, `register` with `credentials: 'include'`.
- Error states for wrong password / duplicate email.

## Out of scope
- Session guard (separate issue).
- Password reset, OAuth.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): session guard + logout in app shell
issues.body:
## Goal
Gate `/` and `/project/*` behind `/v1/me`; add logout.

## Acceptance criteria
- Layout fetches `/v1/me`; unauth redirects to `/login`.
- Logout button in `AppShell`, POSTs `/v1/auth/logout`, redirects.
- Works when `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` unset (skip guard, dev mode).

## Out of scope
- Remember-me.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): provider settings page shell (list + delete)
issues.body:
## Goal
Read-only list of existing provider connections + delete; separate from add-key form.

## Acceptance criteria
- `src/app/settings/providers/page.tsx`.
- `lib/api/provider-connections.ts` with `list`, `remove`.
- Table: provider, label, createdAt, lastUsedAt.
- Delete with confirm modal.

## Out of scope
- Add-key form (separate issue).
- Test-connection button (separate issue).

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): add-provider-key form
issues.body:
## Goal
Let user submit a new API key; key never persisted client-side.

## Acceptance criteria
- Form on `/settings/providers`: provider select, label, api key (password field).
- On submit: POST, clear key from state, refetch list.
- Key never written to localStorage, Redux, or URL.

## Out of scope
- Masking/reveal of stored keys (never returned).

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): test-connection button on provider settings
issues.body:
## Goal
Surface backend connectivity test result.

## Acceptance criteria
- Button per row calls `POST /v1/provider-connections/:id/test`.
- Success/error toast with code + message.
- Disabled while in flight.

## Out of scope
- Auto-test on save.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): wire create-project, create-workspace, create-chat-window to real POSTs
issues.body:
## Goal
Backend POST endpoints exist; UI still mutates local state only.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lib/api/{projects,workspaces,windows}.ts` gain `create*` functions.
- Create flows dispatch POST, refresh from server on success.
- Mock mode preserved when API unset.

## Out of scope
- Rename/delete (separate issue).

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): rename + delete for projects/workspaces/chat-windows
issues.body:
## Goal
Persist rename (currently local-only) and add delete mutations.

## Acceptance criteria
- PUT + DELETE clients + UI affordances.
- Confirm modal before delete.
- Optimistic rename with rollback on error.

## Out of scope
- Trash/undo.

issues.title: [frontend] refactor(web): migrate web to canonical chat-window endpoints
issues.body:
## Goal
Backend standardized on `/v1/chat-windows/*` and `windowIdâ†’chatWindowId`. Web still calls legacy alias paths and uses `windowId` in places.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lib/api/windows.ts` renamed `chat-windows.ts`; updates call sites.
- Local `windowId` fields renamed `chatWindowId` for consistency with backend.
- `pnpm --filter @webapp/web typecheck && test` green.

## Out of scope
- Removing backend aliases (backend task).

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): message metadata display (provider, model, timestamp)
issues.body:
## Goal
Render provider/model/created-at on assistant messages once the loop returns them.

## Acceptance criteria
- Small metadata row under assistant bubbles.
- Handles missing fields (legacy mock messages).
- No layout regression in empty states.

## Out of scope
- Token usage display.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): global error boundary + toast for ApiCallError
issues.body:
## Goal
Surface API failures visibly instead of silent console errors.

## Acceptance criteria
- `components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` wraps root.
- Minimal toast component (no new dep).
- `apiFetch` errors reach toast layer.

## Out of scope
- Retry UI per toast.

issues.title: [frontend] feat(web): Sentry browser SDK init
issues.body:
## Goal
Backend Sentry landed in `87beb1c`; web errors still invisible in prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- `@sentry/nextjs` installed, init in `instrumentation-client.ts`.
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` gated; no-op when unset.
- Error boundary forwards caught errors to Sentry.

## Out of scope
- Session replay, performance tracing.

issues.title: [frontend] test(web): Playwright golden-path e2e
issues.body:
## Goal
Cover auth, create project, workspace, chat, logout against stubbed provider.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/web/e2e/golden-path.spec.ts`.
- CI job `web-e2e` on PRs touching `apps/web/**`.
- README section for local run.

## Out of scope
- Visual regression.

issues.title: [coord] chore(ci): remove || echo fallbacks; make lint/typecheck/test real gates
issues.body:
## Goal
CI currently can't fail. Blocks trustworthy PR gates for agents.

## Acceptance criteria
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: all `|| echo ...` removed.
- `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build` as real steps.
- Any currently-failing step fixed OR a follow-up issue opened per failure.

## Out of scope
- Adding coverage/e2e jobs.

issues.title: [coord] chore(repo): issue + PR templates matching label taxonomy
issues.body:
## Goal
Standardize issue + PR shape so agents self-serve without clarification turns.

## Acceptance criteria
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{backend,frontend,coord}.yml` with Goal / AC / Out-of-scope / Test-plan.
- `.github/pull_request_template.md` mirrors AC checklist + `Closes #`.
- Templates reference label namespaces.

## Out of scope
- Automation.

issues.title: [coord] chore(repo): seed GitHub labels from taxonomy
issues.body:
## Goal
Guarantee every label in the taxonomy exists in GitHub before agents file issues.

## Acceptance criteria
- `tools/seed-labels.sh` using `gh label create --force`.
- Run documented in `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- All labels visible in UI after run.

## Out of scope
- Webhooks.

issues.title: [coord] docs(adr): Track A + Track B merged topology
issues.body:
## Goal
Capture why Track B merged, and how DB-gated routing with in-memory fallback is expected to behave until fallback is removed.

## Acceptance criteria
- `docs/technical/adr/XXXX-track-ab-merge.md`.
- Linked from `project-memory/02-decisions.md`.

## Out of scope
- Removing fallback.

issues.title: [coord] docs(api): flesh out apps/api/README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md
issues.body:
## Goal
Both files are 1-line stubs. Agents need run/env/test instructions + contribution rules.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/api/README.md`: Overview, Run, Env vars, Endpoints link, Tests. <150 lines.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: branch pattern, conventional-commits scopes, label use, memory-update rule.

## Out of scope
- Code of conduct.
